text: Breaking news. This image could show life on Mars. This is real. NASA just said this. This very well could be the clearest sign of life that we've ever found on Mars. The image was taken last year by this Mars rover right here, a place that we're pretty sure used to be filled with water, but it dried up over time. The rover took a sample of that rock and found that those tiny leopard spots contain materials typically left behind by living microbes, like organic carbon and sulfur and iron and phosphate. We're not 100% sure. But a lot of scientists have reviewed this data and they say as of now, we can't find another explanation. Meaning these could be the fingerprints of microbial life left behind on Mars. Huge if true. The next step is they're bringing these samples home from more tests. So if you want to know what happens, follow along.
